entries.openingLines: Many years later, as he faced the firing squad, Colonel Aureliano Buendía was to remember that distant afternoon when his father took him to discover ice.
entries.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
entries.author: Gabriel García Márquez

entries.openingLines: It was a queer, sultry summer, the summer they electrocuted the Rosenbergs, and I didn't know what I was doing in New York.
entries.title: The Bell Jar
entries.author: Sylvia Plath

entries.openingLines: If you really want to hear about it, the first thing you'll probably want to know is where I was born, and what my lousy childhood was like...
entries.title: The Catcher in the Rye
entries.author: J.D. Salinger
